ops.title: 2026-07-16: ship hook gate (ccx v0.26.0) + fleet .pre-commit-config.yaml as cc-guides rendered artifact — design, sweep results, and the hygiene-pass damage patterns
ops.body:
Landed 2026-07-16. `ccx vcs ship` (v0.26.0) runs `uvx prek run --cd <root> --files <derived>` between staging and commit with auto-fix-then-verify (re-stage + re-derive + one retry; empty re-derive = "nothing to commit" error, not an empty jj commit); `--no-verify` skips prek and reaches every git commit argv. jj file derivation is pinned to repo root via `render.RunCLIDir` — jj emits cwd-relative paths, and unpinned a subdir ship silently skipped hooks. Full design + gotchas in cc-notes note `01720869`; deferred repos (cookie-sync, write-like-me — trees held) in backlog task `a54be0c7`.

`.pre-commit-config.yaml` is now a cc-guides artifact fleet-wide (~27 repos): pack fragments `cc-skills plugin/guides/yml/precommit-{base,go,python,swift}.yml`. KindYAML pre-existed my plan (landed same morning for docs workflows, `.yml` canonical + LintYAML validity) — I only added `.yaml` target acceptance (cc-guides v0.1.27). Local fragments can carry TOP-LEVEL keys (concatenation is textual): `exclude: ^tests/fixtures/` is the pattern for byte-exact fixtures.

**Why:** jj commits never fire git hooks, so the fleet's prek lint gate was skipped on the primary commit path (papercut 2026-07-16 12:37); and per-repo configs had no rollout mechanism.

**How to apply:** (1) The `--all-files` hygiene pass is the danger zone: cc-steer (fixture EOF newline broke a byte assertion) and cc-transcript (imported swiftformat rewrote 450+ lines of bridge code with no repo swiftformat config → compile break; golden JSON/JSONL EOF fixes broke pytest) — never let hygiene hooks touch `tests/testdata|fixtures` or an unconfigured swift subtree; back out cosmetic damage wholesale and ship the revert `--no-verify` so the gate can't re-apply it. (2) `repo: builtin` configs are prek-only. (3) Rev pins are central: local `prek autoupdate` = drift, reverted by the daily cron. (4) Codex-wrapper fleet agents: demand synchronous runs AND ≥600s command timeouts; a templated-looking report ("test"/"a.go") means inspect the tree — the work usually landed. Related: [[ship-auto-rebase]], [[cc-guides-fragment-pipeline]], [[guides-fleet-remigration]].
